creator: lightside@gmx.com
text:
Created attachment 166375
Possible patch for armv6 architecture (untested)

I guess, there is a possibility to use GCC compiler on armv6 architecture to fix assimp's configure error, which showed on current PortsMon for 20151130 version:
http://beefy8.nyi.freebsd.org/data/head-armv6-default/p406714_s293048/logs/errors/pioneer-0.0.20151130.log

But I can't test this configuration on newer FreeBSD version for core dump(s), which I see on FreeBSD 10.2, when using GCC, instead of CLANG compiler.

The possible patch attached for armv6 architecture, if there are interested people to test it (build and run).
